text: If I had more time, I would learn the piano.
I wish I had more time.

Make the most of
I wish I lived closer to the sea.
I wish I knew more French.
If only I were a better cook.
Sandra, if you could change one small thing about today, what would you wish for?
Honestly, I wish I had more time. I always daydream about a slow afternoon by the sea.
I understand. Do you ever wonder how life would feel if you lived closer to the water?
I do. If only I lived by the sea. But I am content; my life is full, even if it is imperfect.
That is a lovely way to see it. Is there a talent you wish you had?
I wish I were a better cook. I long for that, but I just make the most of my simple meals.
I wish I had
If only I lived
I wish I were
I wish I were a better cook.
If only I lived closer to the sea.
I sometimes daydream and long for a slower life.
I am content, and I make the most of what I have.

Let me share a few honest wishes of mine. I wish I had more hours in the day. I always feel a little rushed, and I daydream about a slower morning with a long coffee and a good book. I also wish I spoke more languages. I studied French at school, but I forgot most of it, and now I miss it. If only I were a more patient gardener, my little balcony plants would survive. I keep them alive, but barely. None of these are big regrets. They are small wishes, the kind everyone has. I think it is good to name them, gently. When I say I wish I had more time, I am not complaining. I am noticing what matters to me. And once I notice, I try to make the most of the time I actually have.

Here are some of my own wishes. I wish I had started learning English much earlier. I do not regret my life, but I sometimes wonder how it would feel to be fluent already. I wish I lived a little closer to the sea. The city is wonderful, but I long for the sound of water. And, honestly, I wish I were a better cook. My family is kind about it, but I know the truth. None of this makes me unhappy. My life is good and full. These are gentle wishes, not heavy regrets. I am content with who I am, imperfect and curious at fifty-one. If only the days were longer, I would read every book on my shelf. But since they are not, I simply make the most of each evening, one page at a time.

First, think of something in your life right now that you want to be different, like more free time. Then, start your sentence with the words I wish, to show it is a present wish. Next, use a past verb, even though you are talking about now, for example I wish I had. After that, finish the idea clearly, saying what you wish were true. Finally, you can use if only instead of I wish, when the feeling is a little stronger.
